title: Deep Fried Peruvian Lamb Seco de Cordero
description: A halal-friendly twist on the traditional Peruvian lamb stew, seco de cordero, featuring kaffir-lime-leaf and an agemono-deep-fry for a fluffy and umami-packed post-workout dish.

ingredients.name: Kaffir lime leaves
ingredients.notes: fresh or frozen, but not dried

instructions: In a large bowl, combine the lamb shoulder, tomato paste, garlic, ginger, kaffir lime leaves, ancho chili powder, cumin, smoked paprika, and salt. Mix well to coat the lamb evenly.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
Preheat the oil in a deep frying pan or a deep fryer to 375°F (190°C).
Remove the lamb from the marinade, letting any excess liquid drip off. Dredge the lamb in the breadcrumbs, shaking off any excess.
In a shallow dish, mix together the cornstarch and flour. Dredge the lamb in the cornstarch mixture, shaking off any excess.
In the same shallow dish, beat the egg and mix with the panko breadcrumbs. Dip the lamb in the egg mixture, then coat in the panko breadcrumbs, pressing gently to adhere.
Carefully place the breaded lamb in the hot oil. Fry for 5-7 minutes on each side, or until golden brown and cooked through.
Using a slotted spoon, remove the lamb from the oil and place on a paper towel-lined plate to drain any excess oil.
Serve the deep-fried lamb hot with a side of white rice, cilantro, and a squeeze of lime juice. Garnish with parsley and a fried egg, if desired.
Serve with a side of chimichurri sauce or yogurt, if desired.
Note: Traditionally, this dish is served as a stew, but the agemono-deep-fry adds a crispy exterior and a fluffy interior.

Note: To make the chimichurri sauce, combine 1 cup of mayonnaise, 1 cup of chopped cilantro, 1 cup of chopped parsley, 2 cloves of garlic, 1/2 cup of red wine vinegar, and 1/4 cup of olive oil in a bowl. Mix well to combine. Refrigerate for at least 30 minutes before serving.

Note: To make the yogurt sauce, combine 1 cup of plain yogurt, 1 tablespoon of lemon juice, and 1/4 cup of chopped cilantro in a bowl. Mix well to combine. Refrigerate for at least 30 minutes before serving.
